free_text: Isobaric yield curves at A=72 from the spallation of
medium mass isotopes by intermediate energy protons.
Self-supporting target pellets were prepared using a
hydraulic press. Effective target thickness 10-50
mg/cm**2.
For absolute yield determination, targets were placed
in individual stacks with aluminum catcher and monitor
foils.
Calibrated Ge(Li) spectrometers.
Irradiation times 1-5 min. with a beam
intensity of 1-5 microA.
Separation times 4-8 mins.
Yields are the arithmetic mean of 3 replicate
determinations.
Total experimental uncertainty including
random and systemetic error.
Sources of random error are: target alignment,
chemical yield, decay curve fits and parent-daughter
separation  time.
Sources of systematic error include: corrections for
minor isotopic constituents in the target, detector
efficiencies, decay schemes and summing corrections.
For the relative results, the uncertainties  reflect
only random sources of error.
Uncertainty of monitor cross section.
Empirical formulas. Fitted shape parameters of
A=72 isobaric yield curves and logarithmic mass-yield
slopes are reported.
Data taken from tables in reference.
No reply from P.J. Karol to author proof.
Converted from data set O0681 compiled by
CAJaD and updated.
OS. DECAY-DATA corrected in subs.7,36,48,
OS. REACTION in sub.28 corrected
